_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: To decrease serum bilirubin before the initiation of chemotherapy
To access biliary system for further palliative interventions such as stent placement or transhepatic brachytherapy for cholangiocarcinoma
To manage post-operative complications like Failed pancreatico-jejunostomy anastomosis
To manage post-operative complications like Failed bilio-enteric anastomosis
To manage post-operative complications like Duodenal stump insufficiency
To manage post-operative complications like Post-operative leak from extrahepatic bile ducts

clinical_thresholds.note: Relative contraindication, triggers TMS pop-up if found.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission

claim.label: Invoice or barcode of the stent (if used)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission submitted?
Are the reports of the relevant Investigations submitted?
Were the patient's Clinical history/ investigations indicative of the procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) submitted?
Is the invoice or barcode of the stent (if used) submitted?

common_queries: Patient's clinical history or investigations are not indicative of the procedure performed.
International Normalized Ratio (INR) is greater than 1.5 without adequate justification for the procedure.
Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management, and advice for admission are missing or incomplete.
Reports of relevant investigations (CBC/LFT/USG/CT/MRCP) are not submitted.
Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) are missing or incomplete at the time of claim submission.
Invoice or barcode of the stent (if used) is not submitted.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Secondary care facilities with interventional radiology facilities.
clinical_key_pointers: Percutaneous biliary drainage is a primary therapeutic or palliative drainage of bile in patients with biliary obstruction. It is an image-guided procedure which can be performed under fluoroscopy or combined ultrasound and fluoroscopic guidance.
